Tests the typeof operator for undefined values in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the type of two variables, `undefined` and `smth`. It uses the `assert` function to verify that both variables are of type `'undefined'`. The test confirms that the JavaScript environment correctly identifies `undefined` as a type and that `smth`, which is not declared, also evaluates to `'undefined'`. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on validating the behavior of the `typeof` operator in JavaScript.
